content:
# Internal: Transfer Submission Procedure

## Pre-Submission Checks
Before submitting a transfer, confirm the customer and transfer request meet all required eligibility conditions: identity_verified, account_status_active, amount_within_per_transfer_cap, external_daily_limit_not_exceeded_including_this_transfer, available_balance_covers_amount_plus_fee. Do not submit while any condition is unresolved. If the customer asks about limits, fees, delivery timing, or cutoff handling before the transfer is sent, use "Transfer Types Fees And Limits Guide" and "Transfer Cutoff And Delivery Policy" rather than estimating.

## Denial Handling
When more than one issue applies, give the denial reason in this order: identity_not_verified, account_not_active, per_transfer_cap_exceeded, external_daily_limit_exceeded, insufficient_available_balance. Stop at the first applicable reason and explain only the action needed to correct that blocker. For example, do not discuss balance sufficiency until identity, account status, caps, and external daily usage have been evaluated in order.

## Submission Tool Use
Transfers are submitted through the documented write action template submit_customer_transfer_{suffix}. This is a WRITE operation and requires unlock: True. Supply only the required arguments: user_id, account_id, transfer_type, amount, destination_reference, effective_date. If any required argument is missing, ask one focused question and wait; do not infer a destination_reference, effective_date, account_id, transfer_type, or amount from unrelated conversation details.

## Final Review
Immediately before submission, recheck that available balance covers the amount plus any applicable fee and that the external daily limit will not be exceeded including this transfer. If the request involves deposited funds that may not yet be available, consult "Funds Availability Policy" before deciding the balance condition is satisfied.
title: Internal: Transfer Submission Procedure
